BCD and boot.sdi are extracted automatically from the ISO. You may supply a custom boot.wim (custom WinPE); otherwise the one from the ISO is used.
Boot flow: DHCP → TFTP (snponly.efi) → iPXE does DHCP again → receives {url} directly thanks to user-class = "iPXE".
Cancel the running build?\n\nSources stay on disk; the next build will be faster.
Delete script “{filename}”?\nThe file will be removed and the menu regenerated.
Delete server “{name}”?\nIt will be removed from the iPXE menu.
Delete this ISO and its files?
Description shown in the iPXE menu
For Proxmox/QEMU/VMware VMs use snponly.efi — it uses the EFI network drivers (virtio-net, e1000…); ipxe.efi bundles its own drivers (better for bare metal).
Full ISO extract — vmlinuz + initrd auto-detected in casper/
If provided, this release appears in the OS “Other” submenu and chains this script instead of the default boot.
If you provide the ISO, boot files will be extracted automatically.
No custom scripts. Upload a .ipxe file when creating an OS release.

Sources are kept — the next build continues with git pull without recloning everything.
The embed will be generated automatically at build time using the URL below.
These entries appear under Remote servers in menu.ipxe and chain-load the iPXE menu at the given URL. The URL is used as-is with no rewriting.
These paths are defined in the server's .env file.
These scripts are uploaded manually for an OS release and appear in the matching _autres.ipxe submenu. Deleting a script removes the file and regenerates the menu.
This URL is embedded in all generated .ipxe files and must be reachable by PXE clients.
Two options: upload a full ISO (automatic boot file extraction), or provide boot files directly (boot.wim, vmlinuz, etc.) without an ISO.

Ubuntu: the ISO is extracted fully (like Windows) so autoinstall cloud-init can reach packages over HTTP. vmlinuz and initrd are auto-detected in casper/. user-data / meta-data are created under Auto configs.
e.g. prod-web, class-a — letters and digits, hyphens after normalization
kickstart — CentOS / Rocky / Alma / Fedora / ESXi
